mnemonics: DOT: Direct, Operator, Typedef - The three ways to give a value to a struct member
keyPoints: Use the dot operator (.) to access a member of a struct variable
Use the arrow operator (->) to access a member through a pointer to a struct
A struct's members can be assigned individually after declaration or initialized all at once during declaration
commonMistakes: Using the arrow operator (->) on a struct variable instead of a pointer to a struct
Forgetting to allocate memory for a struct pointer before trying to assign values to its members
Trying to assign values to members in the wrong order during initialization, which relies on the struct's definition order
